introSection: World's Largest Portable Roller Coaster was a steel kiddie roller coaster built by Miler Manufacturing located at Playland at the Beach.

sections.title: History
sections.content: World's Largest Portable Roller Coaster was built in the 1960s in the old plot of Big Dipper. It was the last roller coaster to be built at the park. The ride closed with the Playland at the Beach on September 4, 1972.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: The ride had an oval layout with a lift hill and two small bunny hills. The track and supports were a golden yellow.

fullText:
World's Largest Portable Roller Coaster was a steel kiddie roller coaster built by Miler Manufacturing located at Playland at the Beach.

World's Largest Portable Roller Coaster was built in the 1960s in the old plot of Big Dipper. It was the last roller coaster to be built at the park. The ride closed with the Playland at the Beach on September 4, 1972.

The ride had an oval layout with a lift hill and two small bunny hills. The track and supports were a golden yellow.
